---
title: "Recipe 7: Body Size and Pattern Complexity"
output: html_document
date: "2026-05-30"
---
```{r}
library(here)
# ── Recipe 7: Body Size and Pattern Complexity ────────────────
source("https://michaelalfaro.github.io/eeb187-demos/recipes/code/helpers.R")

# ── 1. Load your data ────────────────────────────────────────
traits <- read.csv(here("data/traits_df_78.csv"),
                   stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
bundle  <- load_bundle(here("data/scarinae-78-bundle"))

# Strip specimen codes from tree tips
bundle$tree$tip.label <- gsub("^([A-Za-z]+_[a-z]+).*", "\\1", bundle$tree$tip.label)
# ── 2. Pull body size from FishBase ──────────────────────────
fb <- pull_fishbase(traits$species,
                    fields = list(estimate = c("MaxLengthTL")))


# ── 3. Merge and transform ──────────────────────────────────
merged <- merge(traits, fb, by = "species")
merged$MaxLengthTL <- as.numeric(merged$MaxLengthTL)

# Deduplicate — keep the row with the largest MaxLengthTL per species
merged <- merged[order(merged$species, -merged$MaxLengthTL), ]
merged <- merged[!duplicated(merged$species), ]

merged <- merged[!is.na(merged$MaxLengthTL) & merged$MaxLengthTL > 0, ]
merged$log_size <- log(merged$MaxLengthTL)
cat("Species with size data:", nrow(merged), "\n")

# ── 4. Match to tree ────────────────────────────────────────
matched <- match_tree_data(bundle$tree, merged, species_col = "species")

# Remove duplicate Sparisoma_radians — keep first row
matched$data <- matched$data[!duplicated(matched$data$species), ]
rownames(matched$data) <- matched$data$species

# Prune tree to match cleaned data
matched$tree <- ape::drop.tip(matched$tree,
                               setdiff(matched$tree$tip.label,
                                       matched$data$species))

cat("After dedup:", nrow(matched$data), "species\n")

# Now run PGLS
model <- safe_pgls(m ~ log_size, df = matched$data, tree = matched$tree)

# ── 5. PGLS: complexity vs log body size ─────────────────────
model <- safe_pgls(m ~ log_size, df = matched$data, tree = matched$tree)

# ── 6a. PGLS scatter plot (log body size) ────────────────────
library(ggplot2)

p1 <- ggplot(matched$data, aes(x = log_size, y = m)) +
  geom_point(size = 4, alpha = 0.7, color = "#264653") +
  { if (!is.null(model))
    geom_abline(intercept = coef(model)[1], slope = coef(model)[2],
                color = "#e76f51", linewidth = 1.2) } +
  labs(x = "log(Max Total Length, cm)",
       y = "Pattern Transition Density (m)",
       title = "Pattern complexity vs. body size (PGLS)",
       subtitle = "Log body size for statistical model") +
  theme_poster

print(p1)
ggsave("results/recipe7-pgls-size-vs-m.pdf", p1, width = 10, height = 8)

# ── 6b. Murray's diagnostic plot (raw body size) ────────────
p2 <- ggplot(matched$data, aes(x = MaxLengthTL, y = m)) +
  # Annotate Murray's threshold zones
  annotate("rect", xmin = 0, xmax = 10, ymin = -Inf, ymax = Inf,
           fill = "#e9c46a", alpha = 0.15) +
  annotate("rect", xmin = 10, xmax = 50, ymin = -Inf, ymax = Inf,
           fill = "#2a9d8f", alpha = 0.10) +
  annotate("rect", xmin = 50, xmax = Inf, ymin = -Inf, ymax = Inf,
           fill = "#264653", alpha = 0.10) +
  annotate("text", x = 5, y = Inf, label = "Small\n(<10 cm)",
           vjust = 1.5, size = 5, color = "#e9c46a", fontface = "bold") +
  annotate("text", x = 30, y = Inf, label = "Medium\n(10-50 cm)",
           vjust = 1.5, size = 5, color = "#2a9d8f", fontface = "bold") +
  annotate("text", x = 75, y = Inf, label = "Large\n(>50 cm)",
           vjust = 1.5, size = 5, color = "#264653", fontface = "bold") +
  geom_point(size = 4, alpha = 0.7, color = "#264653") +
  labs(x = "Max Total Length (cm, raw scale)",
       y = "Pattern Transition Density (m)",
       title = "Murray's scaling diagnostic",
       subtitle = "Raw body size to visualize theoretical threshold zones") +
  theme_poster

print(p2)
ggsave("results/recipe7-murray-diagnostic.pdf", p2, width = 12, height = 8)

cat("Saved: results/recipe7-pgls-size-vs-m.pdf\n")
cat("Saved: results/recipe7-murray-diagnostic.pdf\n")
```


```{r}
# Check for duplicates after your dedup step
table(merged$species)[table(merged$species) > 1]

# And check what match_tree_data returns
table(matched$data$species)[table(matched$data$species) > 1]
```
